steps.title: Use custom Desmos regression
steps.content: Every answer choice has the form $h(x)=2^x+c$, so this is a one-parameter regression problem. Enter the table as $x_1=[0,1,2]$ and $y_1=[17,18,20]$, then type $y_1 \sim 2^{x_1}+c$. Desmos returns $c=16$, so the model is $h(x)=2^x+16$.

steps.title: Eliminate the choices
steps.content:
• Choice A — '$h(x) = 2^x + 16$': $h(0) = 1 + 16 = 17$ ✓
  • Choice B — '$h(x) = 2^x + 15$': $h(0) = 1 + 15 = 16 eq 17$ ✗
  • Choice C — '$h(x) = 2^x + 2$': $h(0) = 1 + 2 = 3 eq 17$ ✗
  • Choice D — '$h(x) = 2^x$': $h(0) = 1 eq 17$ ✗
That matches choice A — A is correct.
